message:
feat(cli): add memory privacy commands for cross-agent settings (ntm-xpje)

Add ntm memory privacy subcommands to wrap cm privacy CLI:
- status: Show cross-agent privacy settings
- enable: Enable cross-agent enrichment
- disable: Disable cross-agent enrichment
- allow <agent>: Allow specific agent
- deny <agent>: Remove agent from allowlist

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ntm-wgxo","title":"Task: Implement PID file management","description":"# Task: Implement PID File Management

Manage PID files for daemon ownership tracking.

## Files to Create/Update
- internal/supervisor/pidfile.go

## PID File Location
.ntm/pids/<name>-<session>.pid

## File Format
```json
{
  "pid": 12345,
  "started": "2026-01-03T10:00:00Z",
  "session": "myproject",
  "owner": "ntm-1234"
}
```

## Key Functions
```go
func WritePIDFile(path string, pid int, session string) error
func ReadPIDFile(path string) (*PIDInfo, error)
func RemovePIDFile(path string) error
func IsOwnedByUs(path string) bool
func ListPIDFiles(dir string) ([]PIDInfo, error)
```

## Ownership Rules
- Only kill daemons we started (check owner field)
- Stale PID files cleaned up on supervisor start

## Tests
- Test write/read cycle
- Test ownership detection
- Test stale cleanup

## Acceptance
- [ ] PID files written on daemon start
- [ ] PID files read correctly
- [ ] Ownership tracked and checked
- [ ] Stale files cleaned up","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:22:12.134253-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T22:51:35.166828-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T22:51:35.166828-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented in internal/supervisor/supervisor.go - health checks in monitorDaemon(), PID files in writePIDFile()/removePIDFile()","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-wgxo","depends_on_id":"ntm-bpxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:22:21.703458-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-wgxo","depends_on_id":"ntm-5qxw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:22:22.23841-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"ntm-wm0c","title":"Display profile names in dashboard","description":"## Overview
Show profile names in the dashboard instead of generic pane names.

## Location
Modify `internal/tui/dashboard/` components

## Current Display
```
┌─ cc_1 ─┐  ┌─ cc_2 ─┐  ┌─ cc_3 ─┐
│ tokens │  │ tokens │  │ tokens │
│ status │  │ status │  │ status │
└────────┘  └────────┘  └────────┘
```

## With Profiles
```
┌─ architect ─┐  ┌─ implementer ─┐  ┌─ tester ─┐
│ cc_1        │  │ cc_2          │  │ cc_3     │
│ tokens      │  │ tokens        │  │ tokens   │
│ status      │  │ status        │  │ status   │
└─────────────┘  └───────────────┘  └──────────┘
```

## Implementation
1. Read profile from pane title or metadata
2. Use profile name as card header
3. Show agent ID (cc_1) as subtitle
4. Profile description in tooltip/hover

## Dashboard Pane Card Changes
```go
type PaneCard struct {
    AgentID     string // cc_1
    AgentType   string // claude
    Profile     string // architect (if assigned)
    // ...existing fields
}

func (c *PaneCard) RenderHeader() string {
    if c.Profile != "" {
        return c.Profile  // Show profile name prominently
    }
    return c.AgentID  // Fallback to agent ID
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Profile name shown as primary identifier when assigned
- [ ] Agent ID shown as secondary info
- [ ] Works with mixed profile/non-profile agents
- [ ] Styling distinguishes profiled vs non-profiled agents
- [ ] No visual regression when no profiles used","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:10:55.924403476-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T19:10:55.924403476-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-wm0c","depends_on_id":"ntm-j8t0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:11:12.132760719-06:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-wm0c","depends_on_id":"ntm-66cp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:11:13.970592886-06:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-wotk","title":"Phase -1: Foundations (Prerequisites)","description":"# Foundational Infrastructure

These components MUST be built FIRST because they enable all Tier 0 integrations to be implemented faster and safer.

## Why Foundations First?
Without these, every integration becomes a one-off that:
- Duplicates tool detection logic
- Hardcodes daemon management
- Uses ephemeral in-memory state
- Polls repeatedly instead of event-driven UIs

## Components

### 1. Tool Adapter Framework (internal/tools/)
- Detect(), Version(), Capabilities(), Health() for each tool (bv, bd, am, cm, cass, s2p)
- Schema guards for JSON responses to detect drift
- Automatic fallback when capabilities are missing
- Cache results per session

### 2. Daemon Supervisor (internal/supervisor/)
- Port allocation + PID files
- Log capture to .ntm/logs/
- Health checks + exponential backoff restart
- Clean shutdown on session end

### 3. Durable State Store (internal/state/)
- SQLite schema for sessions, agents, tasks, reservations, messages
- Correlation IDs for traceability
- Enables crash recovery and re-attach

### 4. Event Bus (internal/events/)
- Pub/sub for session lifecycle events
- Subscriptions for TUI, robot API, web dashboard
- Replay from event log for crash recovery

### 5. ntm doctor Command
- Tool detection (bv, bd, am, cm, cass, s2p)
- Version compatibility checks
- Daemon health verification
- tmux version and configuration
- PATH wrapper status

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All tools have adapters with Detect/Version/Capabilities/Health
- [ ] Supervisor can start/stop/restart daemons with PID tracking
- [ ] State store persists sessions, agents, tasks, reservations
- [ ] Event bus supports pub/sub with at-least-once delivery
- [ ] ntm doctor validates entire ecosystem health","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:12:46.989167-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T22:27:34.234834-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T22:27:34.234834-05:00","close_reason":"All 5 foundation components complete: Tool Adapter Framework (internal/tools/), Daemon Supervisor (internal/supervisor/), Durable State Store (internal/state/), Event Bus (internal/events/), ntm doctor (internal/cli/doctor.go). Phase -1 complete - Tier 0 integrations now unblocked.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-wotk","depends_on_id":"ntm-kmz2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:14:01.223269-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-ws2","title":"Create internal/output package with JSON/text formatters","description":"Create a unified output formatting system that all commands will use.

## What This Task Does

Create a new internal/output package that provides:
1. OutputFormat type (JSON, Text, Table)
2. Formatter interface that commands implement
3. Root-level --json flag handling
4. Consistent JSON schema patterns

## Why This Is First

This is the foundation for all JSON output. Every command will use this
package, so it needs to be designed well upfront.

## Technical Details

Package structure:
internal/output/
├── format.go      # OutputFormat enum, Formatter interface
├── json.go        # JSON-specific formatting helpers
├── text.go        # Text-specific formatting (existing patterns)
└── schema.go      # Common JSON schema types

Key types:
type OutputFormat int
const (
    FormatText OutputFormat = iota
    FormatJSON
)

type Formatter interface {
    Format(data interface{}, format OutputFormat, w io.Writer) error
}

Common JSON patterns:
- Timestamps as ISO 8601 strings
- Errors include 'error' field with message
- Lists are always arrays (even empty)
- Nested objects for complex data

## Implementation Steps

1. Create internal/output directory
2. Define OutputFormat enum and detection logic
3. Create Formatter interface
4. Add JSON helper functions (marshal with indent, error wrapping)
5. Add root --json flag to cobra root command
6. Create context passing for format flag

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] internal/output package exists with clean API
- [ ] --json flag added to root command
- [ ] Format detection works (flag, env var, pipe detection)
- [ ] JSON output is consistently formatted
- [ ] Package is documented with examples

 {"id":"ntm-x0h","title":"Unit Tests: internal/tmux package (5.4% → 80%)","description":"# Unit Tests: internal/tmux Package

## Current State
- Coverage: 5.4%
- Tests: 4 existing (session name validation, agent type detection)

## Test Approach
Use REAL tmux sessions. Each test creates a unique session and cleans up after.

## Test Cases

### session.go

#### Session Management
- [ ] TestCreateSession - creates real session, verifies it exists
- [ ] TestCreateSessionWithDir - verifies working directory is set
- [ ] TestCreateSessionDuplicate - error handling for existing session
- [ ] TestKillSession - kills session, verifies gone
- [ ] TestSessionExists - true/false for existing/non-existing

#### Pane Operations
- [ ] TestSplitWindow - creates new pane, returns correct ID
- [ ] TestSplitWindowMultiple - creates 5 panes, verifies indices
- [ ] TestSetPaneTitle - sets title, verifies with list-panes
- [ ] TestGetPanes - lists all panes with correct metadata
- [ ] TestCapturePaneOutput - captures recent output

#### Layout Operations
- [ ] TestApplyTiledLayout - tiles panes, verifies layout
- [ ] TestSelectPane - selects specific pane
- [ ] TestZoomPane - zooms and unzooms

#### Key Sending
- [ ] TestSendKeys - sends text, verifies in pane output
- [ ] TestSendKeysWithEnter - sends text with enter key
- [ ] TestSendInterrupt - sends Ctrl+C

#### Agent Type Detection
- [x] TestAgentTypeFromTitle - existing test, expand coverage
- [ ] TestAgentTypeFromTitle_EdgeCases - empty, special chars

#### Attachment
- [ ] TestAttachSession - attach to session (difficult to test)
- [ ] TestSwitchToSession - switch client to session

### Helper Functions
- [ ] TestInTmux - detect if running in tmux
- [ ] TestIsInstalled - detect tmux installation

## Test Helpers Needed
```go
func createTestSession(t *testing.T) string {
    name := fmt.Sprintf("ntm_test_%d", time.Now().UnixNano())
    t.Cleanup(func() { KillSession(name) })
    CreateSession(name, os.TempDir())
    return name
}
```

## Skip Conditions
- Skip all tmux tests if tmux not installed
- Skip attach tests if not in tmux","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:20:02.989397-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:26:55.911298-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T03:26:55.911298-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-x0h","depends_on_id":"ntm-idx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:22:33.599967-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-xkvu","title":"Implement file modification tracking per agent","description":"# Implement File Modification Tracking Per Agent

## Purpose

Track which files each agent modifies, enabling conflict detection and contribution attribution.

## Approach

### Method 1: File Mtime Tracking
```go
type FileSnapshot struct {
    Path  string
    Mtime time.Time
    Size  int64
}

func SnapshotDirectory(dir string) map[string]FileSnapshot {
    // Walk directory, record mtime/size for each file
}

func DetectChanges(before, after map[string]FileSnapshot) []FileChange {
    // Compare snapshots, return changed files
}
```

### Method 2: Git Status Parsing
```go
func GetUncommittedChanges() ([]string, error) {
    // git status --porcelain
    // Parse output for modified/added/deleted files
}
```

### Attribution to Agents
Track changes with timestamps:
```go
type AgentChange struct {
    Agent     string    // Pane identifier
    File      string
    Timestamp time.Time
    ChangeType string  // modified, added, deleted
}
```

When `ntm send` completes, snapshot files again and attribute changes to the targeted agents.

## Limitations

- Can't perfectly attribute if multiple agents running simultaneously
- Best-effort: attribute to agent(s) that were sent prompts most recently
- Works best with sequential workflows

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Snapshot file state before prompts
- [ ] Detect changes after prompts complete
- [ ] Attribute changes to agents (best-effort)
- [ ] Store change history for comparison
- [ ] Handle git-ignored files appropriately","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:29:14.145569-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T21:21:36.627594-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T21:21:36.627594-05:00"}
 {"id":"ntm-xle","title":"Implement cross-platform clipboard detection and access","description":"Create internal/clipboard package for cross-platform clipboard access.

## What This Task Does

Create a clipboard abstraction that works on macOS, Linux (X11/Wayland),
and WSL. This provides the foundation for clipboard-related features.

## Platform Detection

Detect platform and available clipboard tools:

macOS:
- pbcopy / pbpaste (always available)

Linux X11:
- xclip -selection clipboard
- xsel --clipboard

Linux Wayland:
- wl-copy / wl-paste

WSL:
- clip.exe (write only)
- powershell.exe Get-Clipboard (read)

Fallback:
- tmux's internal clipboard (with warning about limitations)

## Implementation

package clipboard

type Clipboard interface {
    Copy(content string) error
    Paste() (string, error)
    Available() bool
}

func New() (Clipboard, error) {
    switch runtime.GOOS {
    case "darwin":
        return &macOSClipboard{}, nil
    case "linux":
        if isWSL() {
            return &wslClipboard{}, nil
        }
        if isWayland() {
            return &waylandClipboard{}, nil
        }
        return &x11Clipboard{}, nil
    }
    return &tmuxClipboard{}, nil
}

## Error Handling

- Tool not found: Clear error with installation suggestion
- Permission denied: Suggest running with appropriate access
- Empty clipboard: Return empty string, not error

## Testing

Need to handle:
- CI environments (no display, no clipboard)
- SSH sessions (no local clipboard)
- Container environments

Skip clipboard tests or mock in these environments.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Clipboard works on macOS
- [ ] Clipboard works on Linux X11
- [ ] Clipboard works on Linux Wayland
- [ ] Clipboard works on WSL
- [ ] Graceful fallback when tools missing
- [ ] Clear error messages

 {"id":"ntm-xz26","title":"Phase 0: Critical Tier 0 - Highest Priority","description":"# Critical Tier 0 Integrations (Highest Priority)

These integrations have ZERO current usage despite being designed specifically for agent coordination. They represent the highest-impact improvements with relatively low effort.

## Why Tier 0?
All of these are features that ALREADY EXIST in the ecosystem tools but NTM simply doesn't use them. Implementation is mostly about calling the right APIs and wiring them into the spawn/assign workflow.

## Integrations

### 1. Agent Mail Macros (Very Low Effort, Critical Impact)
- macro_start_session: One call replaces 4-5 separate calls
- macro_prepare_thread: Align agent with existing thread
- Immediate benefit: Faster spawning, fewer error points

### 2. BV Mega-Commands (Very Low Effort, Critical Impact)
- -robot-triage: Single call returns insights + priority + plan + alerts
- Replaces current pattern of 4 separate bv calls
- Immediate benefit: Faster analysis, less code

### 3. Destructive Command Protection (Low Effort, Critical Impact)
- Auto-install safety hooks during spawn
- Block git checkout --, git reset --hard, rm -rf
- Log all blocked commands for audit
- Prevents data loss incidents

### 4. File Reservation Lifecycle (Low Effort, Critical Impact)
- Reserve files before assigning work
- Release files after completion
- Force-release stale with approval workflow
- Pre-commit guard installation
- Prevents multi-agent conflicts

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Spawn uses macro_start_session (4-5 calls → 1)
- [ ] Triage uses -robot-triage (4 calls → 1)
- [ ] Safety hooks auto-install during spawn
- [ ] File reservations wrap all work assignments
- [ ] Blocked commands logged to .ntm/logs/blocked.jsonl","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:13:07.933989-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-04T13:16:11.351405-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T13:16:11.351405-05:00","close_reason":"All 4 Tier 0 integrations complete: Agent Mail Macros (macro_start_session, macro_prepare_thread), BV Mega-Commands (--robot-triage, --robot-next), Destructive Command Protection (policy engine with blocked/approval_required patterns), File Reservation Lifecycle (force-release with validation, renew with TTL extension). Phase 0 complete - Phase 1 now unblocked.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-xz26","depends_on_id":"ntm-wotk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:14:00.472521-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
